content.plain_english: This week in Year 4 maths, your child is working on Length and perimeter. The learning focuses on: estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence; estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence; and measure and calculate the perimeter of a rectilinear figure (including squares) in centimetres and metres. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: Children use practical measuring equipment — rulers, scales, measuring cylinders, and clocks — to explore measurement in real contexts. They record findings using diagrams and number lines before progressing to written calculations. Problem-solving tasks show how these skills are used every day.
content.home_activity: Use a piece of string to measure things around the house — is the sofa longer or shorter than the table? Estimate in centimetres first, then measure to check. For perimeter, measure all four sides of a book or tile and add them up.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence?
Was it easy or tricky to estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: The total distance around the outside edge of a shape.
How long something is from one end to the other.
How wide something is from side to side.
A unit of length — 100 cm = 1 metre.
A unit of length — 1000 m = 1 kilometre.

example_questions: Find the area of a rectangle 8 cm × 6 cm.
Find the perimeter of a square with side 9 cm.
A rectangle has perimeter 28 cm and width 5 cm. Find its length.
content.times_table_tip: Year 4 children should know all their times tables to 12 × 12 by the end of the year — the national Multiplication Tables Check happens in Year 4. Daily 3-minute practice mixing tables in random order is the best preparation.
